The '''Y-4 Transport''' was a military [[Transport/Legends|transport]] manufactured by [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]].<ref name="Cracken" /> Quite common during the [[Hunt for Zsinj|Zsinj campaign]], they came to be so synonymous with [[Raptors|Zsinj's Raptors]] that the vessels were called '''Y-4 Raptors''' in common conversation.<ref name="GuideV&V" />

==Characteristics==
[[File:Y4Raptor-CTD.png|thumb|left|Side view of the transport]]
A sturdy ship that was designed to move quickly and hit hard, the Y-4 was equipped with a pair of [[S-foils/Legends|retractable wings]] for emergency braking and increased maneuverability.<ref name="Cracken" /><ref name="GuideV&V" /> Combined with meter-thick heat-dispersive armor and multiple layers of spray-on heat ablative coatings, the Y-4 could make full use of its impressive speed without having to slow down due to atmospheric friction.<ref name="Cracken" /><ref name="GuideV&V" /> For this reason, the Y-4 was popular with [[Zsinj]]'s [[Raptors]] for quick and quiet insertion onto a planet's surface.<ref name="Cracken" /><ref name="GuideV&V" />

Just under thirty meters long, the Y-4 had a small command crew of just one [[pilot]] and two [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]], and was capable of carrying forty troops, four [[All Terrain Scout Transport|AT-STs]], and several [[Compact Assault Vehicle/Wheeled PX-10|PX-10 CAVws]] and [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|military speeder bikes]]; folding assault ramps allowed both soldiers and vehicles to depart in seconds.<ref name="Cracken" /><ref name="GuideV&V" /> A modest weapons platform, the Y-4 was armed with a pair of dual [[laser cannon]]s and a [[concussion missile]] launcher for defense, and was able to provide copious cover for its occupants as they disembarked.<ref name="Cracken" /><ref name="GuideV&V" />

==Civilian variant==
The Y-4 was available in a civilian model which lacked weaponry and armor plating, and was slower than the military version, though was still considered fast enough to perform as a [[Courier/Legends|courier]]. While it was capable of [[hyperspace]] travel, the threat of [[pirate]]s and other hazards forced many owners to upgrade their civilian Y-4s with weaponry for protection. Civilian-grade Y-4s were widely used as maintenance and supply vessels by Warlord Zsinj.<ref name="GuideV&V" />

==History==
[[File:Y4Raptor_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Y-4 ''Raptor'' [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]]]]
Introduced to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] shortly before Incom's [[Imperialization/Legends|nationalization]], the Y-4 never saw widespread use due to Imperial prejudice against the alleged [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] sympathies of its creators.<ref name="Cracken" /><ref name="GuideV&V" />

The Rebel Alliance used a Y-4 at a [[Escape from Fest|rendezvous point]] on [[Fest/Legends|Fest]]. Three captured [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|All Terrain Personal Transports]] escaped aboard the transport.<ref name="Star Wars: Rogue Squadron video game">[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]</ref>

The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] captured several Y-4 transports in their campaign against Zsinj, and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] even appropriated one of their transponder codes for use on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].
